page_content: HNUH278Y Science in an Age of Truthiness (3 Credits) Scientiﬁc research has the power to advance understanding, create new technologies, and improve our lives. And yet scientiﬁc language - which is essential for these achievements - can be appear opaque and untrustworthy to non-scientists. Indeed, the fact that scientiﬁc understanding develops over time can even make the knowledge seem capricious. As a result, science is both unfairly maligned and unrealistically praised, sometimes even in the same breath. Through both the philosophy of science and historical scientiﬁc literature, we will survey how scientists have done and expressed science. Students will be empowered to critically evaluate current conceptions of science as these are revealed in the debates around climate change and COVID-19. Credit Only Granted for: HONR299I or HNUH278Y. Formerly: HONR299I.
